name: Cocrystal Pharma, Inc.
business: Cocrystal Pharma, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company discovering and developing novel antiviral therapeutics as treatments for serious and/or chronic viral diseases. The company employs structure-based drug design and Nobel Prize-winning expertise to develop small molecule inhibitors targeting RNA viruses including influenza, norovirus, coronaviruses, and hepatitis C virus.
sellsTo: Patients with influenza, norovirus, coronavirus, and hepatitis C infections

exposures.sentence: Cocrystal Pharma, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company discovering and developing novel antiviral therapeutics as treatments for serious and/or chronic viral diseases.

catalysts.event: Received FDA Study May Proceed Letter for Phase 1b challenge study of CDI-988
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025 we received a Study May Proceed Letter from the FDA to conduct a Phase 1b challenge study in the U.S. evaluating CDI-988 as a norovirus preventive and treatment.

catalysts.event: Commencement of Phase 1b challenge study of CDI-988 at Emory University
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we announced commencement of the Phase 1b challenge study at Emory University School of Medicine.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 1 billion cases of seasonal influenza worldwide annually, with 3-5 million severe illnesses and up to 650,000 deaths.
tamClaims.sentence: Each year there are approximately 1 billion cases of seasonal influenza worldwide, with 3-5 million severe illnesses and up to 650,000 deaths, according to the World Health Organization.

tamClaims.claim: In the U.S. alone, noroviruses are responsible for an estimated 21 million cases annually, including 109,000 hospitalizations, 465,000 emergency department visits and an estimated 900 deaths.
tamClaims.sentence: In the U.S. alone, noroviruses are responsible for an estimated 21 million cases annually, including 109,000 hospitalizations, 465,000 emergency department visits and an estimated 900 deaths, according to the CDC.

tamClaims.claim: COVID-19 has caused more than 777 million reported cases globally and more than 7 million deaths as of February 2026.
tamClaims.sentence: COVID-19 is a global health concern responsible for more than 777 million reported cases globally, including more than 7 million deaths, as of February 2026, according to data reported by the WHO.

tamClaims.claim: 50 million people worldwide have chronic HCV infections with about 1 million new infections per year.
tamClaims.sentence: The 2024 World Health Organization Global Hepatitis Report estimates that 50 million people worldwide have chronic HCV infections with about 1 million new infections occurring per year and an estimated 3.2 million adolescents and children with chronic HCV infection.

tamClaims.claim: Influenza is responsible for approximately $10.4 billion in direct medical costs in the U.S. annually.
tamClaims.sentence: In addition to the health risk, influenza is responsible for approximately $10.4 billion in direct medical costs in the U.S. annually, according to the National Institutes of Health.

tamClaims.claim: Norovirus annual burden to the United States is $10.6 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: The NIH estimates the annual burden to the United States at $10.6 billion.
